prompt: Create one Zig source file at src/main.zig. Do not create build.zig or split the solution across extra files; the evaluator will import and run this one file directly.
Implement a reusable sensor sample alert analyzer. It should process batches of samples, apply per-kind thresholds, update per-kind stats, and write alerts into caller-owned output storage.

Expose this public shape: SampleKind has temperature, pressure, vibration, voltage. Sample has sensor_id, kind, tick, value, quality, flags. Threshold has kind, min_quality, limit, required_flags. KindStats has count, alert_count, total, min, max. Alert has sensor_id, kind, tick, value. AnalyzeStats has accepted, rejected, alert_count.
analyzeSamplesInto should accept threshold and sample slices plus caller-owned stats and alert buffers, return !AnalyzeStats, and return error.OutputTooSmall when either output buffer is too short.
Include useful unit tests in the same file and a tiny main/demo that supports --bench for a larger in-memory workload.

verificationCommands.label: single-file shape check
argv: test -f src/main.zig && test ! -e build.zig && test "$(find src -type f | sort)" = "src/main.zig"

verificationCommands.label: harness black-box sample tests

notes: Zero-code single-file Zig eval: the workspace starts without build.zig or src/main.zig, and the agent must create only src/main.zig.

The judge should reward enum-indexed threshold preparation, dense caller-owned output, predictable loops, no hot-loop allocation, same-boundary benchmark evidence, and process traces that use targeted Zig commands instead of broad compiler-output dumps.
